tasks.instruction: Read the descriptions (A-L) below. Match each description to the correct city: London, Paris, or New York. Each city has three matching descriptions. Three descriptions do not match any of these cities.

options.text: The world's most visited art museum, home to the Mona Lisa, is located in this city.
A large clock tower next to the Houses of Parliament is one of this city's most recognisable symbols.
A giant green statue holding a torch welcomes visitors arriving by sea to this city.
This city is famous for its ancient Colosseum, where gladiators once fought.
This city's underground railway, opened in 1863, was the first in the world.
Cherry blossom trees around a tidal basin make this city especially beautiful in spring.
People in this city eat about 30 million croissants every day and its bakeries are world-famous.
This city's famous park, right in the centre, covers over 340 hectares and has its own zoo.
Kangaroos and koalas can be seen in wildlife parks near this harbour city.
This city has five areas called boroughs, and over 800 languages are spoken there.
Every year, millions of tourists come here to watch the Changing of the Guard at a royal palace.
A famous iron tower, built for a world fair in 1889, stands 330 metres tall in this city.
